title: The space outside
checkLabels.t10: You centred the box, not the text
checkLabels.t11: The heading is still dark red
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start over.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. The cards still touch.
hint2.untouched: One new line in the .card rule.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. There are two cards and there stay two.
why.no-card: There is no element with the card class.
hint2.cards-touch: You do not need more room INSIDE. You need room OUTSIDE the box.
hint3.cards-touch: Padding widens the box itself — the border moves outward with it. A margin does not touch the box, it pushes the neighbour.
why.width-changed: You changed the width. It stays 248.
hint2.width-changed: A margin does not count towards the size of the box, so nothing needs recalculating.
why.padding-cut: You deleted the padding. It is for the inside, the margin is for the outside — one does not replace the other.
why.not-centered: The cards still sit against the left edge.
hint2.not-centered: There is a value that tells the browser to split the leftover space itself.
hint3.not-centered: auto on the left and right means: take whatever space is left over and divide it evenly between the two sides. It only works when the element has a width — otherwise there is no leftover to divide.
why.used-text-align: You used text-align: center. That centres the text INSIDE the box while the box stays where it was.
hint2.used-text-align: Look at the preview: the letters moved to the middle but the border did not budge. The box itself has to move.
why.h1-broken: The heading is no longer dark red.
blocks.0.text: Two cards, one under the other. They are stuck together, their borders merge into one thick line and you cannot see where the first one ends. Padding does not help — that gives room inside, between the text and the border, and the problem is between the two borders.
blocks.2.text: Padding is space INSIDE the element: increase it and the box grows, pushing the border outward. A margin is space OUTSIDE it: the box stays the same and the neighbours move away. That is why a margin does not count towards the size of the box, and why nothing needs recalculating when you add one.
blocks.3.text: padding grows inward and pushes the border. margin leaves the box alone and pushes the neighbour.
blocks.5.text: One, two or four numbers, exactly like padding — and the same order: top, right, bottom, left. Or one side at a time:
blocks.7.text: There is one difference you will need: a margin accepts negative numbers. margin-top: -8px pulls the element up, over its neighbour. It is rare and usually a sign that something else is wrong, but it exists. Negative padding is simply not accepted.
blocks.9.text: This is the margin's second job:
blocks.11.text: auto means: let the browser work it out. On the left and right that means take the leftover space and split it evenly. It only works when the element has a width — otherwise it fills everything and there is no leftover to split. And do not confuse it with text-align: center, which arranges the letters inside the box and does not move the box. The two look similar at first glance and solve different problems.
blocks.13.text: Put 16 pixels between the two cards and centre them horizontally. The width, padding and border stay as they are — nothing needs recalculating.
